---
title: "Home"
---

::: {.hero-block}
# Connor Conkey-Morrison

**PhD Candidate | Provisional Psychologist | Sessional Academic**

Research on school attendance problems, emotionally based school avoidance, and adolescent mental health. My work brings together intervention evidence, longitudinal modelling, and population-level education data to better understand why students disengage from school and what responses are most effective.
:::

::: {.button-row}
[Research](research.qmd){.btn-link}
[Publications](publications.qmd){.btn-link}
[Teaching & Roles](teaching.qmd){.btn-link}
[CV](cv.qmd){.btn-link}
[Contact](contact.qmd){.btn-link}
:::

## Profile

I am a PhD candidate at RMIT University and a provisional psychologist. My research focuses on school attendance problems, with a particular interest in emotionally based school avoidance, intervention evidence, developmental patterns of disengagement, and the ways attendance is represented in large-scale administrative data.

Alongside research, I work across teaching, student support, and school-based practice.

## Current affiliations

- **RMIT University**  
  PhD Candidate, School of Health and Biomedical Sciences

- **CatholicCare Victoria**  
  Provisional Psychologist / Industry partnership

- **RMIT University and Cairnmillar Institute**  
  Sessional teaching and academic support

## Selected links

- [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0009-0000-1020-2333)
- [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ZEvUL_kAAAAJ&hl=en)
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/connorconkeymorrison)
- [PROSPERO protocol](https://www.crd.york.ac.uk/PROSPERO/view/CRD420251089918)
